description_long: This cathedral features a grand, elongated nave with a steeply pitched green roof and prominent flying buttresses along its sides. The structure is dominated by a tall, pointed spire at one end, flanked by two smaller towers, all with sharp, Gothic-style pinnacles. The exterior walls are dark, likely stone, with vertical columns and arched windows that suggest a classic Gothic architectural influence. The apse at the rear is semi-circular, surrounded by radiating chapels and additional buttresses. The roofline is punctuated by smaller spires and gables, adding to the vertical emphasis of the design. Decorative elements are present but not overly ornate, focusing on structural embellishments like arches and columns.
description: A large, Gothic-style cathedral with a tall spire, green pitched roof, flying buttresses, and arched windows. The structure is dark stone with vertical columns and a semi-circular apse.
description_view_0: Front view showing the tall central spire, flanked by two smaller towers and the main entry portal beneath.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the long nave, repetitive flying buttresses, and the length of the green roof.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the semi-circular apse, radiating chapels, and the symmetry of the twin towers.
description_view_3: Angled side view showing both the spire and the apse, with clear visibility of the buttresses and roof structure.
attribute_facade_ornamentation: The main facade features pointed arches, vertical columns, and minimal sculptural ornamentation. Decorative tracery and structural embellishments are present but not overly elaborate.
attribute_entry_portal_design: The main entry portal is a pointed arch with vertical columns and a large central door. Decorative features are restrained, focusing on the arch shape and structural elements.
